title: Pilot

jokes.text: So does he have a hump and a hairpiece?
jokes.setup: Friends are trying to figure out what's wrong with Monica's date
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific physical deformities as dealbreakers
jokes.explanation: Classic Friends rapid-fire absurdist questioning. Gets laugh through specificity of 'hump and hairpiece' combo.

jokes.text: Wait, does he eat chalk?
jokes.setup: Building on the absurd dealbreakers theme
jokes.punchline: Even more random and specific potential flaw
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from physical flaws to bizarre behavior. 'Chalk eating' is wonderfully random.

jokes.text: It's just two people going out to dinner and not having sex.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to downplay that it's a date
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describes exactly what a date is while denying it's a date
jokes.explanation: Great structural irony - Monica inadvertently defines dating while claiming it's not a date.

jokes.text: Sounds like a date to me.
jokes.setup: Monica's elaborate non-date description
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry, obvious response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler delivery - cutting through Monica's denial with simple logic.

jokes.text: Then I look down and I realize there is a phone.. there. - Instead of - That's right!
jokes.setup: Ross describing his naked dream
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to genitalia being replaced by phone
jokes.explanation: Awkward innuendo delivered in Ross's typically awkward style. The hesitation sells it.

jokes.text: They weren't looking at you before?
jokes.setup: Ross says everyone starts looking at him in the dream
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out he was already naked
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler logic joke - pointing out the obvious flaw in Ross's story with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Which is very, very weird because she never calls me.
jokes.setup: Ross reveals the caller in his dream was his mother
jokes.punchline: The weird part isn't the phone-genitals but that mom called
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ross focuses on the wrong aspect of his bizarre dream, revealing his relationship with his mother.

jokes.text: He says, 'Hello,' I want to kill myself.
jokes.setup: Ross describing his emotional state after Carol left
jokes.punchline: Extreme suicidal ideation triggered by simple greeting
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that establishes Ross's depression while getting laughs from the extreme reaction to mundane interaction.

jokes.text: I feel like someone pulled my intestine out of my mouth.. and tied it around my neck. - Cookie?
jokes.setup: Ross gives graphic description of emotional pain
jokes.punchline: Monica cheerfully offers cookie as if nothing happened
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition - visceral metaphor immediately undercut by mundane hospitality. The timing is everything.

jokes.text: Just leave my aura alone, okay? - Fine, be murky.
jokes.setup: Ross rejecting Phoebe's spiritual help
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's petulant response using her own mystical terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - takes his rejection personally and responds with passive-aggressive mystical language.

jokes.text: You never knew she was a lesbian?
jokes.setup: Ross's marital problems are being discussed
jokes.punchline: Joey bluntly asks the obvious awkward question
jokes.explanation: Joey's characteristic lack of tact creates uncomfortable but funny moment. Simple but effective character beat.

jokes.text: She didn't know. How should I know?
jokes.setup: Everyone fixating on Ross not knowing Carol was lesbian
jokes.punchline: Ross points out the logical flaw in their questioning
jokes.explanation: Good logical comeback - if Carol didn't know she was gay, how could Ross be expected to know?

jokes.text: Sometimes I wish I was a lesbian. Did I say that out loud?
jokes.setup: Rachel processing the lesbian revelation
jokes.punchline: Accidentally voices inappropriate thought then realizes her mistake
jokes.explanation: Classic 'did I say that out loud' moment. Works because it's relatable awkwardness.

jokes.text: So that hysterical phone call from a woman sobbing.. 'I'll never have grandchildren,' was what? A wrong number?
jokes.setup: Ross claims parents took the news well
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals specific evidence contradicting his claim
jokes.explanation: Well-structured reveal that undercuts Ross's denial with specific, vivid detail about their mother's reaction.

jokes.text: Strip joints! Hey, you're single. Have some hormones.
jokes.setup: Ross expressing pain about being single
jokes.punchline: Joey's typically crude but enthusiastic solution
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey - solving emotional problems with sex. 'Have some hormones' is a great Joey-ism.

jokes.text: And I just want a million dollars!
jokes.setup: Ross saying he just wants to be married again
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out that wanting something doesn't make it happen
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deflation - bringing Ross's romantic idealism down to earth with practical cynicism.

jokes.text: You want to tell us now, or are we waiting for four wet bridesmaids?
jokes.setup: Rachel appears in wedding dress, clearly having fled wedding
jokes.punchline: Monica asks if they need to wait for the typical rom-com entrance
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke referencing movie tropes. 'Four wet bridesmaids' is wonderfully specific visual.

jokes.text: I realized I was more turned on by this gravy boat than Barry.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she left the wedding
jokes.punchline: Finding kitchenware more sexually appealing than her fiancé
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - the specificity of 'gravy boat' makes it hilarious and memorable.

jokes.text: How much Barry looks like Mr. Potato Head. I always knew he looked familiar, but..
jokes.setup: Rachel continuing to explain her wedding revelation
jokes.punchline: Comparing fiancé to children's toy
jokes.explanation: Visual comparison joke that creates specific mental image. The 'always knew he looked familiar' sells the realization.

jokes.text: Tuna or egg salad! Decide!
jokes.setup: Rachel on phone with her father
jokes.punchline: Father's aggressive sandwich ordering in crisis moment
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - shows father's controlling nature through mundane food ordering.

jokes.text: I'll have whatever Christina's having.
jokes.setup: Rachel ordering at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Reference to famous 'When Harry Met Sally' line
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference without much twist or character integration. Gets by on recognition.

jokes.text: If I let go of my hair, my head will fall off.
jokes.setup: Rachel stressed and holding her head
jokes.punchline: Absurd physical explanation for stress gesture
jokes.explanation: Nice physical comedy moment - takes literal interpretation of stress gesture to absurd conclusion.

jokes.text: Push her down the stairs! Push her! Push her down the stairs!
jokes.setup: Watching soap opera character in wedding dress
jokes.punchline: Monica's violent enthusiasm for soap opera drama
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's investment in TV drama. The repetition and escalation sells the joke.

jokes.text: All my life, everyone's always told me, 'You're a shoe!' 'You're a shoe!' What if I don't want to be a shoe? What if I want to be a purse? Or a hat?
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to explain her life choices to her father
jokes.punchline: Using increasingly absurd accessories metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist metaphor that perfectly captures Rachel's confusion about identity through fashion accessories.

jokes.text: You can see where he'd have trouble.
jokes.setup: Rachel's confusing shoe/purse/hat metaphor
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation about father's confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment - cutting through Rachel's emotional metaphor with logical observation.

jokes.text: Wait! I said maybe!
jokes.setup: Rachel declaring independence from father's money
jokes.punchline: Immediately backtracking when reality hits
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Rachel's privileged background - independence sounds good until money disappears.

jokes.text: Raindrops on roses And whiskers on kittens... La la la something And noodles with string
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to calm Rachel with 'My Favorite Things'
jokes.punchline: Completely mangling the Sound of Music lyrics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - well-intentioned but completely wrong. The 'noodles with string' is wonderfully off.

jokes.text: I helped.
jokes.setup: Rachel feels better after Phoebe's mangled song
jokes.punchline: Phoebe takes credit despite getting lyrics completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect two-word punchline. Phoebe's innocent pride in her 'help' is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: Like there's a rule, or something?
jokes.setup: Being told not to hit on Rachel because it's her wedding day
jokes.punchline: Joey genuinely confused about wedding day dating etiquette
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey - genuinely doesn't understand social conventions around appropriate timing for hitting on women.

jokes.text: Please don't do that again. It's a horrible sound.
jokes.setup: Someone makes an unpleasant noise (buzzer)
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt assessment of the sound
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction joke. Gets mild laugh from Monica's directness about annoying sound.

jokes.text: Does he sell it, drink it? Or he just complains a lot?
jokes.setup: Paul being described as 'the wine guy'
jokes.punchline: Chandler plays on multiple meanings of 'wine guy'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'wine' vs 'whine' - classic Chandler linguistic humor with good structure.

jokes.text: I didn't catch your name. Paul?
jokes.setup: Paul was just introduced as Paul
jokes.punchline: Chandler pretends not to have heard the obvious
jokes.explanation: Chandler being deliberately obtuse for comedic effect. Works through his deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: I just pulled out four eyelashes. That can't be good.
jokes.setup: Monica nervous about her date
jokes.punchline: Specific nervous habit with self-aware commentary
jokes.explanation: Relatable nervous behavior with good specific detail. Monica's self-awareness adds to the humor.

jokes.text: She really likes it when you rub her neck in the same spot.. until it starts to get red.
jokes.setup: Joey giving dating advice about Monica
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately specific and potentially painful advice
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey inappropriate overshare. The specificity of 'until it gets red' makes it awkwardly funny.

jokes.text: Although, Aruba. This time of year? Talk about your.. big lizards.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to make conversation about Rachel's honeymoon destination
jokes.punchline: Trailing off into random, nonsensical observation about lizards
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward Chandler moment - starts confident then realizes he has nothing to say about Aruba except 'big lizards.'

jokes.text: I wish I could, but I don't want to.
jokes.setup: Asked to help with furniture moving
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's brutally honest refusal
jokes.explanation: Iconic Phoebe line - perfect honesty disguised as politeness. Became widely quoted for its perfect economy and truth.

jokes.text: Love is sweet as summer showers Love is a wondrous work of art But your love Oh, your love, your love Is like a giant pigeon Crapping on my heart
jokes.setup: Phoebe singing romantic-sounding song
jokes.punchline: Romantic lyrics turn crude and absurd with pigeon metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe song - starts romantic then takes sharp turn to absurdist imagery. 'Giant pigeon crapping' is memorable.

jokes.text: I have no bracket-y thing. I see no worm guys whatsoever.. and I cannot feel my legs.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to assemble furniture with incomprehensible instructions
jokes.punchline: Escalating from missing parts to physical distress
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from technical problems to physical comedy. Ross's made-up terminology is endearing.

jokes.text: I would have to say that is an L-shaped bracket. - Which goes where? - I have no idea.
jokes.setup: Chandler identifies furniture part with authority
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits complete ignorance about its purpose
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline structure. Chandler's confident identification followed by complete cluelessness.

jokes.text: She always drank it out of the can. I should have known.
jokes.setup: Ross reflecting on missed signs about Carol being lesbian
jokes.punchline: Drinking beer from can as somehow indicative of sexual orientation
jokes.explanation: Ross desperately looking for signs he missed. The absurdity of the 'evidence' makes it funny.

jokes.text: I should've known when she went to the dentist five times a week. I mean, how clean can teeth get?
jokes.setup: Ross listing signs he missed about Carol
jokes.punchline: Finally a legitimate clue that he completely missed
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from silly signs to obvious one. Ross's obliviousness is perfectly captured.

jokes.text: Say her - Leg?
jokes.setup: Discussion of accidentally breaking something valuable of Carol's
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests breaking her leg instead of possessions
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that escalates from property damage to bodily harm. Joey's casualness sells it.

jokes.text: You probably think it's about making love with your socks on, but it isn't.
jokes.setup: Rachel leaving message for Barry about why she left
jokes.punchline: Awkward intimate detail shared in breakup message
jokes.explanation: TMI moment that reveals both Rachel's privileged naivety and Barry's quirks. Uncomfortable but relatable.

jokes.text: I'm divorced. - I'm only 26, and I'm divorced! - Shut up!
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel comparing life crises
jokes.punchline: Rachel's age-inappropriate outrage interrupted by Monica
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's self-absorption and Monica's no-nonsense response.

jokes.text: We haven't had a relationship that's lasted longer than a Mento.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining why he and Joey avoid serious relationships
jokes.punchline: Using candy as unit of measurement for relationship duration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comparison - using how long it takes to eat a Mento as relationship duration metric. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Unfortunately, in my case, there was only one woman for her.
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether there's only one person for everyone
jokes.punchline: Ross's bitter twist on the romantic notion
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that turns romantic cliché on its head. Captures Ross's pain with clever structure.

jokes.text: That's like saying there's only one flavor of ice cream. Let me tell you something. There's lots of flavors out there. Rocky road and cookie dough and bing cherry vanilla. You can get them with jimmies or nuts or whipped cream.
jokes.setup: Joey countering the 'one person for everyone' theory
jokes.punchline: Extended food metaphor that reveals Joey's priorities
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey - turns relationship advice into food enthusiasm. The specific flavors and toppings show his character.

jokes.text: Welcome back to the world. Grab a spoon!
jokes.setup: Joey's ice cream metaphor for dating
jokes.punchline: Encouraging Ross to dive into dating with ice cream imagery
jokes.explanation: Great callback that extends the ice cream metaphor. 'Grab a spoon' becomes motivational dating advice.

jokes.text: I don't know if I'm hungry or horny. - Then stay out of my freezer.
jokes.setup: Ross confused by ice cream-dating metaphor
jokes.punchline: Monica's quick comeback protecting her actual ice cream
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline with Monica's practical response to Ross's metaphorical confusion.

jokes.text: It's more of a fifth date kind of revelation.
jokes.setup: Monica hesitant to share something personal
jokes.punchline: Treating emotional sharing like a dating milestone
jokes.explanation: Relatable modern dating humor - categorizing intimacy levels by date number.

jokes.text: Ever since she left me.. I haven't been able to perform.. sexually.
jokes.setup: Ross about to reveal something personal
jokes.punchline: Awkward confession of sexual dysfunction on first date
jokes.explanation: Classic TMI moment that's both uncomfortable and humanizing for Ross. The hesitation sells the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Being spit on is probably not what you need right now.
jokes.setup: Monica accidentally spits while reacting to Ross's confession
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on her inappropriate physical reaction
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy combined with Monica's awareness of her poor timing. Adds levity to heavy moment.

jokes.text: We are gathered here to join Joanie Louise Cunningham.. and Charles. Chachi, Chachi, Chachi..
jokes.setup: Wedding scene on TV
jokes.punchline: Happy Days characters getting married
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference that parallels Rachel's wedding situation. Gets by on recognition value.

jokes.text: But Joanie loved Chachi. That's the difference.
jokes.setup: TV wedding comparison to Rachel's situation
jokes.punchline: Rachel identifies the key difference - actual love
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's growing self-awareness about her own relationship.

jokes.text: Do the words, 'Billy, don't be a hero,' mean anything to you?
jokes.setup: Asked about grabbing a spoon (dating)
jokes.punchline: Reference to old song about avoiding dangerous situations
jokes.explanation: Somewhat obscure pop culture reference that's more clever than funny. Shows Rachel's cultural knowledge.

jokes.text: Andrea's the screamer. Angela has cats.
jokes.setup: Joey confused about which woman he's dating
jokes.punchline: Identifying women by sexual behavior or pet ownership
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey moment - reducing women to basic identifying characteristics. Crude but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I think it's, 'If I can invade Poland, there's nothing I can't do.'
jokes.setup: Rachel's innocent coffee-making confidence
jokes.punchline: Chandler references Nazi invasion for comparison
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor that deflates Rachel's innocent confidence. Typical Chandler cynicism.

jokes.text: Oh, good. Lenny and Squiggy are here.
jokes.setup: Joey and Chandler arriving
jokes.punchline: Comparing them to Laverne & Shirley characters
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that gets mild laugh from comparison to annoying TV neighbors.

jokes.text: Last night was like all my birthdays, both graduations.. plus the barn-raising scene in Witness.
jokes.setup: Paul describing great sex with Monica
jokes.punchline: Comparing sex to Amish barn-raising scene
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd escalation ending with specific movie reference. The Witness reference is wonderfully random.

jokes.text: What the hell do you do on a real date?
jokes.setup: Realizing Paul's night was apparently not a real date
jokes.punchline: Monica's shocked reaction to implications
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier 'not a date' theme with Monica's horrified realization about the implications.

jokes.text: Unless you saw the Wee One's production of Pinocchio.. at the little theater in the park? - It was a job!
jokes.setup: Joey describing his acting credits
jokes.punchline: Defensively justifying children's theater work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - embarrassed about children's theater but defensive. 'It was a job!' shows his desperation.

jokes.text: Once I was a wooden boy A little wooden boy
jokes.setup: Mocking Joey's Pinocchio role
jokes.punchline: Chandler singing in character
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy as Chandler mocks Joey. Gets laugh from Chandler's willingness to embarrass himself to tease Joey.

jokes.text: You look like you slept with a hanger in your mouth.
jokes.setup: Rachel won't stop smiling
jokes.punchline: Monica's specific visual comparison for fixed smile
jokes.explanation: Great visual metaphor for an unnaturally fixed smile. The specificity of 'hanger' makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Well, it's like that. With feelings.
jokes.setup: Comparing current romance to past fling
jokes.punchline: Simple but effective distinction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective way to distinguish between casual and serious relationships. Rachel's growing sophistication.

jokes.text: I'm pushing my aunt through Parrot Jungle, you're having sex.
jokes.setup: Franny's vacation vs Monica's sex life
jokes.punchline: Stark contrast between boring family duty and exciting romance
jokes.explanation: Good contrast joke showing the difference in their vacations. 'Parrot Jungle' is a wonderfully specific detail.

jokes.text: Before me, there was no snap in his turtle for two years.
jokes.setup: Franny revealing she also slept with Paul
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to erectile dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Creative euphemism for sexual dysfunction. 'Snap in his turtle' is memorably weird phrasing.

jokes.text: Is it like I have some sort of beacon that only dogs.. and men with emotional problems can hear?
jokes.setup: Monica wondering why she attracts problem men
jokes.punchline: Comparing herself to dog whistle for damaged men
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware metaphor about attracting the wrong type of men. The dog whistle comparison is clever.

jokes.text: They're my 'I don't need a job.. I've got great boots' boots.
jokes.setup: Rachel bought expensive boots while unemployed
jokes.punchline: Naming boots after their delusional purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rachel moment showing her privileged disconnect from financial reality. The specific naming is endearing.

jokes.text: I know that. That's why I was getting married.
jokes.setup: Being told she can't live off her parents
jokes.punchline: Revealing marriage was her financial plan
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest admission about marriage motivations. Shows Rachel's privileged background and lack of independence.

jokes.text: I ended up living with this albino guy who was cleaning windshields. And then he killed himself. Then I found aromatherapy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe sharing her difficult past
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning suicide then pivoting to aromatherapy
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe dark history delivered matter-of-factly. The casual transition from suicide to aromatherapy is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: The word you're looking for is: 'Anyway..'
jokes.setup: Phoebe's traumatic story leaves everyone speechless
jokes.punchline: Monica provides the socially appropriate transition word
jokes.explanation: Perfect response to Phoebe's overshare. Monica's social awareness provides the awkward transition everyone needed.

jokes.text: Rachel, that was a library card.
jokes.setup: Rachel ceremonially cuts up what she thinks is a credit card
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals she cut up the wrong card
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Rachel's dramatic gesture. Shows her inexperience with practical matters.

jokes.text: If you listen closely, you can hear a thousand retailers scream.
jokes.setup: Rachel cutting up credit card (or trying to)
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dramatic description of economic impact
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler observation about Rachel's shopping impact on the economy. Good escalation from personal to societal.

jokes.text: Welcome to the real world! It sucks. You're gonna love it.
jokes.setup: Rachel taking first steps toward independence
jokes.punchline: Monica's contradictory welcome to adulthood
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction that captures adult life. Became highly quotable for its honest cynicism about growing up.

jokes.text: You probably didn't know this, but in high school.. I had a major crush on you. - I knew. - You did? - I figured you thought I was Monica's geeky brother. - I did.
jokes.setup: Ross admits high school crush, thinks it was secret
jokes.punchline: Rachel knew and confirms he was indeed geeky
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of Ross's romantic revelation. Rachel's brutal honesty about his geeky status is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: I just grabbed a spoon.
jokes.setup: Ross asking what's up with him after successful interaction with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Ross references Joey's earlier ice cream dating metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Ross taking Joey's dating advice to heart. Great character growth moment with callback payoff.

jokes.text: I said you had a nice butt. It's just not a great butt.
jokes.setup: Joey defending his assessment of someone's appearance
jokes.punchline: Making subtle distinctions about butt quality
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey moment - inappropriately detailed about physical attributes. The distinction between 'nice' and 'great' is absurd.

jokes.text: Could you give this to that guy over there? - Go ahead. Thank you. - Sorry. Okay, Las Vegas. I'm in Las Vegas. I'm Liza Minnelli..
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing a new dream
jokes.punchline: Casually transitioning to being Liza Minnelli in Vegas
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe absurdist dream scenario. The Liza Minnelli twist is wonderfully random and character-appropriate.

tmdb_overview: Rachel leaves her fiancé at the altar and moves in with Monica, discovering that independence without parental support presents unexpected challenges.
